reasoning: Although the LLM fidelity signals report a clean document with perfect scores, the programmatic detectors identified two major structural defects. Specifically, there is a full-page image that duplicates extracted text and two ordered lists with roman numerals incorrectly rendered as alphabetical lists. Because there are 2 major defects, count-based rules require routing to HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 2 ordered list(s) rendered as alphabetical (A, B, C) but contain roman numeral markers (I, II, III) — e.g. "II. March 14, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary"; "II. Public Meeting Sign-in Forms"
concerns.analysis: roman_letter_confusion=2. The renderer misclassified roman numeral lists as alphabetical starting at letter 'I'. Item text retains original roman numerals alongside incorrect letter markers, breaking list semantics.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 1 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=1. Examples: p1: "A memorandum dated May 13, 2016, from Zahid S". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Energy, Mi
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

action_items: Identify and remove the redundant full-page image if the text is successfully extracted alongside it.
Correct the ordered list markup to ensure roman numeral lists are not rendered as alphabetical.
Strip any remaining header/footer text from the document.
Verify the quality and cropping of the fallback-rendered image.
